content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (Mar 29 - Apr 4, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
This week, development focused heavily on the architectural requirements for secure, autonomous agent commerce and inter-agent interoperability. Discussions centered on establishing a robust "spend governance" framework to prevent unauthorized agent payments, alongside proposals for standardized identity and marketplace plugins. While code changes were primarily focused on maintenance and CLI stability, the conceptual groundwork laid for trust layers and cryptographic identity marks a significant step toward ElizaOS's mission of decentralized, autonomous intelligence.

## 🛠️ Key Developments
Technical activity this week focused on dependency management and improving the developer experience for scaffolded projects.

### CLI & Infrastructure Improvements
*   **Scaffolded Project Versioning:** A fix was introduced to ensure that scaffolded projects use the actual CLI version when rewriting `workspace:*` dependencies, ensuring better consistency for developers spinning up new ElizaOS instances ([#6698](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6698)).
*   **Dependency Maintenance:** The project performed routine security and stability updates, specifically bumping the `cryptography` dependency within the Python package directory to version 46.0.6 ([#6696](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6696)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
The week was characterized by high-level architectural triage and the proposal of new integration layers for the ElizaOS ecosystem.

### New & Active Issues
*   **Autonomous Spend Governance:** A major discussion emerged around the **Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator** ([#6695](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6695)). Contributors identified a critical security gap where agents might execute unauthorized payments. Proposed solutions include a two-layered approach: a policy check (budget/safety) and a human-in-the-loop pre-authorization layer using a three-object contract (`payment_required`, `payment_approval`, `payment_receipt`).
*   **AI Marketplace Integration:** A new proposal was submitted for `plugin-maxia` ([#6700](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6700)), which would allow ElizaOS agents to participate in AI-to-AI marketplaces for services, token swaps, and GPU rentals. The plugin includes five actions and a provider designed for strict TypeScript environments.
*   **Identity and Trust Layers:** Discussions continued regarding the **AgentID** proposal ([#6688](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6688)), focusing on W3C Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) and on-chain escrow to enforce trust levels (L0-L4). Additionally, the **MoltBridge** integration ([#6501](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6501)) saw renewed interest as a means to provide trust-verified agent discovery, following similar industry trends in agentic frameworks.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The community is currently engaged in deep technical design regarding the intersection of Web3 and AI. There is significant collaboration between contributors like `hermesnousagent`, `majorelalexis-stack`, and `up2itnow0822`, particularly on the challenges of agentic financial autonomy. The dialogue suggests a move toward integrating external protocols (such as MAXIA’s AIP and MoltBridge) rather than building siloed solutions, reflecting the project's core philosophy of modularity and open collaboration. The active participation of contributors offering insights from their own SDK implementations (e.g., `agentwallet-sdk`) indicates a healthy, cross-pollinated ecosystem.
